packageText: === MASTER SOURCE PACKAGE === TITLE: Chronic Subdural Hematoma CANONICAL_HIERARCHY_JSON: ["All Categories", "Basic", "Neuroradiology", "Brain", "Primary Effects of CNS Trauma", "Chronic Subdural Hematoma"] CANONICAL_DECK_PATH: Corebook::Neuro::Brain::Primary Effects of CNS Trauma::Chronic Subdural Hematoma SOURCES: RadPrimer canonical backbone; STATdx supplemental depth. SOURCE_PAIRING: None / chronic_subdural_hematoma === SOURCE USE SUMMARY === - [Both] Chronic subdural hematoma is chronic (> 3 weeks to months) subdural blood product; mixed acute-on-chronic hemorrhage is common. - [RadPrimer] The RadPrimer hierarchy is the canonical routing backbone for deck path, package breadcrumb, and downstream IMAIOS grouping. - [STATdx] Supplemental additions retained here include recurrence risk factors (age > 75 years, anticoagulation, liver disease, obesity), delayed contrast diffusion into a cSDH, additional ossified/calcified examples, dural thickening/enhancement, and management wording that middle meningeal artery embolization is becoming a primary therapy. - [Both] No separate Core Radiology evidence was provided in this bundle; do not treat any claim as Core-verified from this master package alone. === FUSED ARTICLE === KEY FACTS - [Both] Chronic subdural hematoma (cSDH) is chronic (> 3 weeks to months) subdural blood products; mixed chronic and acute hemorrhage is common. - [Both] Imaging pattern: crescent-shaped extraaxial collection spreading diffusely over the affected hemisphere, often with enhancing membranes, septations/loculations, fluid-fluid levels, recurrent mixed-age hemorrhage, and inward displacement/compression of brain, cortical vessels, and sulci. - [Both] NECT is a good initial screen; wide window settings (150-200 HU) help identify small cSDH; MR better demonstrates membranes, organization, loculation, rebleed, sequence-dependent blood products, and diffusion restriction in vascularized membranes. - [Both] Main differential: subdural hygroma, subdural effusion, subdural empyema; broader mimics include pachymeningopathies, dural tumors/metastases, and chemical-shift artifact. - [Both] Pathology: serosanguineous fluid encapsulated by granulation tissue/neomembranes with fragile neocapillaries; a minority are multiloculated with fluid-blood density levels. - [Both] Older age and brain atrophy are major risks; recurrence varies by internal architecture and residual volume. - [STATdx] Additional recurrence risk factors include age > 75 years, anticoagulation therapy, liver disease, and obesity. TERMINOLOGY - [Both] cSDH means a chronic collection of blood products in the subdural space, usually older than 3 weeks and sometimes containing foci of newer hemorrhage. IMAGING - [Both] Best diagnostic clue: crescent-shaped, multiseptated extraaxial collection with enhancing surrounding membranes, diffuse hemispheric spread, and mass effect on the underlying brain surface. - [Both] Location: between inner dura and arachnoid; supratentorial convexity is most common; collection may cross sutures but not dural attachments and may extend along falx or tentorium. - [Both] NECT: density varies with blood age. Chronic collections are often CSF density, but cSDH may increase in density or size between 3 weeks and 3 months because fragile outer-membrane neocapillaries rebleed. Long-standing collections can calcify. - [Both] CECT/T1 postcontrast: inward displacement of enhancing cortical vessels plus enhancement of dura, outer/inner membranes, or surrounding pachymeninges. - [STATdx] Delayed contrast diffusion into a cSDH can make an otherwise chronic collection appear isodense; follow-up can show clearing of contrast and better delineation of septations. - [Both] MRI: T1/T2 signal varies with age, protein, and rebleed. FLAIR is usually sensitive because cSDH is often hyperintense to CSF. T2*/GRE or SWI shows blooming blood products. DWI may show restricted diffusion in vascularized membranes, suggesting fresh hemorrhage risk and recurrent bleeding tendency. DIFFERENTIAL DIAGNOSIS - [Both] Subdural hygroma: CSF-like collection after surgery/trauma with no blood, no encapsulating membranes, and no enhancement. - [Both] Subdural effusion: plasma exudate, often after meningitis, not simple CSF. - [Both] Subdural empyema: pus in the subdural space with peripheral enhancement and central restricted diffusion. - [Both] Pachymeningopathies: chronic meningitis, postsurgical/shunt-related change, intracranial hypotension, sarcoid, and other dural thickening processes may mimic membranes. - [Both] Tumors: meningioma, lymphoma/leukemia, metastases, and especially breast/prostate/melanoma dural metastases can produce dural-based enhancement and sometimes SDH. - [Both] Chemical-shift artifact from fat can mimic T1-hyperintense SDH, particularly with high field of view or low bandwidth. PATHOLOGY AND MECHANISM - [Both] Traumatic stretching/tearing of bridging cortical veins initiates SDH; chronic SDH evolves over 2-3 weeks and can enlarge, stabilize, or resolve. - [Both] Growth and recurrence involve inflammation in the hematoma cavity, angiogenesis in the capsule, hyperfibrinolysis, and fragile capillary bleeding in the outer membrane. - [Both] Internal architecture includes homogeneous/laminar, separated/gradated with hematocrit levels, and trabecular forms with septa and thickened or calcified capsule. - [Both] Surgical/pathologic correlate: encapsulated cavity with outer and inner membranes; strip-like/column adhesions divide cavities and provide a substrate for recurrent hemorrhage. CLINICAL ISSUES AND MANAGEMENT - [Both] Presentation ranges from asymptomatic to depressed consciousness; symptoms reflect mass effect, diffuse brain injury, and secondary ischemia. - [Both] Older adults are most at risk; cSDH is the most common intracranial hemorrhage type in the elderly/older adult population. - [Both] Approximately 15% recur; recurrence is higher with separated internal architecture, bilateral collections, large preoperative collections, skull-base location, and residual postoperative volume > 80 mL. - [STATdx] Clinical recurrence risk factors include age > 75 years, anticoagulation, liver disease, and obesity. - [Both] Burr-hole drainage is classic but recurrence is common; drainage plus subdural drain, membrane/septation management, and endoscopic approaches may reduce recurrence in selected cases. - [Both] Devascularization by middle meningeal artery embolization targets the vascularized membranes. - [STATdx] Middle meningeal artery embolization is becoming a primary therapy in appropriate cSDH cases. DIAGNOSTIC CHECKLIST - [Both] Use enhancement to help separate chronic SDH membranes from pachymeningopathies. - [Both] If there is no trauma history, consider underlying vascular lesion or dural metastasis. === IMAGE COVERAGE GATE === - STATdx exact-duplicate coverage of RadPrimer images: 20/22. - STATdx conceptual replacement but not exact coverage: RadPrimer image 10 has calcified/ossified STATdx analogs, but no same cephalad armored-brain slice. - Not covered by STATdx: RadPrimer image 15, the 6-week post-MMA-embolization follow-up timepoint. - Archive rule applied: keep all RadPrimer backbone images primary; archive only STATdx same-image/same-slice duplicate exports. === PRIMARY TEACHING IMAGE SET === - RadPrimer image 1: Axial graphic depicts pathology of classic chronic subdural hematoma (SDH) with the formation of thin internal , thicker external membranes. Note the stretched bridging veins crossing the SDH, top-to-bottom gradation of fluid from serosanguinous to more proteinaceous contents. - RadPrimer image 2: Axial NECT depicts chronic SDH as a crescentic, hypodense extraaxial fluid collection over the left hemisphere. Stretched cortical veins are faintly visible as they cross the SDH. - RadPrimer image 3: Axial graphic depicts "mixed" SDH. Numerous loculations that contain hemorrhages of multiple ages are present. Note the fluid-fluid levels with mixed acute, subacute blood . Rehemorrhage into preexisting subacute &/or chronic SDH is common. - RadPrimer image 4: Axial NECT shows classic left-sided "mixed" SDH . Note the multiple loculations and fluid-fluid levels formed by acute rehemorrhage into preexisting loculated chronic SDH. - RadPrimer image 5: Axial NECT in an elderly patient with multiple falls shows a "bubbly" fluid collection with loculations and multiple blood-fluid levels . - RadPrimer image 6: Axial CTA in the same patient shows the "bubbly" crescentic fluid collection. Note the inward displacement of brain with compressed sulci and displaced cortical veins . - RadPrimer image 7: Axial T2* GRE MR in the same patient shows multiple hypointense blooming foci, consistent with hemorrhage into a chronic loculated SDH. Note multiple locules with blood-fluid levels . - RadPrimer image 8: Axial T1 C+ FS MR shows some enhancement around the margins of the chronic SDH , consistent with a membrane of organizing granulation tissue. - RadPrimer image 9: Axial NECT in a patient with Dandy-Walker malformation shows a bilateral chronic SDH with calcified margins . The brain is squeezed toward the midline by the SDH. - RadPrimer image 10: More cephalad NECT in the same case shows margins of the loculated chronic SDHs are heavily calcified . This unusual appearance is sometimes termed "armored brain." - RadPrimer image 11: This case illustrates the concept of devascularizing membranes of chronic SDHs by embolizing the middle meningeal arteries. This 78-year-old man had recurrent chronic SDHs, shown on this NECT as mixed-density SDHs . - RadPrimer image 12: Lateral DSA of the selective left external carotid artery in the same patient shows the normal middle meningeal artery . The superficial temporal and maxillary arteries are also opacified. - RadPrimer image 13: DSA of the left external carotid artery in the same patient after embolization of the middle meningeal artery (MMA) shows the superficial temporal and maxillary arteries. The MMA has been occluded and is not opacified. - RadPrimer image 14: Axial NECT in the same patient 2 weeks after the left MMA embolization shows the left mixed a/cSDH has become progressively more hypodense. The right cSDH is also slightly more hypodense compared to the initial NECT. - RadPrimer image 15: Axial NECT 6 weeks after the left MMA embolization shows the left cSDH continues to decrease in density and is slightly smaller. The right chronic SDH has almost completely resolved. - RadPrimer image 16: Axial NECT 5 months later shows both chronic SDHs have almost completely resorbed, and there is no evidence for recurrent hemorrhage. One year after MMA embolization, the patient is normal and has had no recurrence of SDHs. - RadPrimer image 17: Axial T1 FS MR in a 79-year-old woman with recurrent right SDH is windowed to show the multiple variable-sized locules of mixed-age blood with fluid-fluid levels . - RadPrimer image 18: Axial T2 MR shows multiple loculated pockets within the chronic SDH. There is significant compression of the underlying sulci and gyri. Note the presence of a thick membrane seen here as a linear, slightly irregular hypointensity outside of the loculated collection. - RadPrimer image 19: Axial T2* GRE MR shows multiple blooming dependent blood-fluid levels within the chronic SDH . Note the thickened, irregular vascularized membrane also shows gradient susceptibility. - RadPrimer image 20: Axial T1 C+ FS MR shows some enhancement in the sulci underlying the chronic SDH, most likely representing compressed vasculature. Because the mixed chronic SDH contains large amounts of subacute hemorrhage and dilute free methemoglobin, it is difficult to determine if any enhancement is present. - RadPrimer image 21: Axial DWI MR through the cephalad part of the chronic SDH shows restricted diffusion in the vascularized membrane. - RadPrimer image 22: ADC confirms restriction in the membrane of the chronic SDH. - STATdx image 5: Coronal CT of a patient who had IV contrast earlier the same day for CT neck. There is delayed contrast diffusion into the chronic right SDH , which appears predominantly isodense to parenchyma. - STATdx image 6: Follow-up coronal CT in the same patient with clearing of the previously noted contrast within the cSDH is shown. The internal septation is now better delineated. - STATdx image 7: Axial T2 MR in the same patient with chronic right cerebral SDH with multiple loculated pockets within the cSDH is shown. There is underlying cerebral compression and sulcal effacement. - STATdx image 8: Axial FLAIR MR with predominantly hyperintensity of the loculated chronic right SDH is shown. - STATdx image 9: Axial SWI MR in the same patient with a web/net appearance of the chronic right SDH with multiple hypointense blooming foci, consistent with hemorrhage into a chronic loculated SDH, is shown. - STATdx image 10: Axial T1 postcontrast MR in the same patient shows thickened peripheral enhancement of the SDH with inward displacement of the cortical veins . - STATdx image 16: A different patient with prior bilateral MMA coil embolization with radiopaque coils along the sphenoid wings is shown. The MMA enters the middle cranial fossa through the foramen spinosum and is the dominant supply to the dura mater. - STATdx image 18: Axial bone CT shows bilateral chronic subdural collections with dense calcifications along the inner membranes . These calcified SDHs are often seen in patients with chronically shunted hydrocephalus, as in this case. - STATdx image 23: Axial NECT shows a cSDH with surrounding neomembranes and internal septations . - STATdx image 24: Axial NECT shows a hemispheric cSDH, largely of CSF density with an interval rebleed producing a separated appearance or hematocrit level. - STATdx image 25: Axial NECT shows bilateral chronic SDHs . Note the foci of acute hemorrhage into the periphery of a loculated right-sided collection, which itself is more recent than the underlying, very hypodense cSDH. - STATdx image 26: Axial NECT shows multiple chronic ossified SDHs . - STATdx image 27: Coronal T1 C+ MR shows a cSDH and extensive dural thickening and enhancement . (Courtesy M. Fruin, MD.) - STATdx image 34: Axial FLAIR is dramatic, showing that the hyperintense loculations contain numerous hypointensities layering in the dependent portions of the pockets. The appearance suggests acute on chronic ("mixed") SDH. - STATdx image 35: Coronal T1 MR in the same patient shows that the loculated pouches of fluid are mostly hyperintense. A few hypointense pockets of more acute hemorrhage are present within the loculations. - STATdx image 36: Axial bone CT shows peripheral calcifications of these chronic bilateral SDH . Patient had been shunted for many years. === ARCHIVE OPTIONAL DUPLICATES === - STATdx image 1: exact duplicate export of RP-01 based on actual image_evidence visual inspection. - STATdx image 2: exact duplicate export of RP-02 based on actual image_evidence visual inspection. - sourceAttributionRules: Use [RadPrimer] for content supported only by RadPrimer.
Use [STATdx] for supplemental detail or unique examples from STATdx.
Use [Both] for concordant points present in both packages.
Use human-facing image labels such as RadPrimer image 5 and STATdx image 24 in prose; keep RP-05/SDX-24 in traceability fields.

textPlan.canonicalBackbone: Use RadPrimer source package as the article scaffold and metadata.json canonicalHierarchy/canonicalDeckPath for all routing.
textPlan.statdxSupplement: Use STATdx for recurrence risk factors, management wording that MMA embolization is becoming primary therapy, delayed contrast diffusion, expanded CT/MR variants, calcified/ossified examples, and dural enhancement examples.
textPlan.downweightOrSkip: Do not duplicate identical overlapping article sections; do not invent Core Radiology validation because no separate Core evidence is provided.

sourceSelectionPlan.imageCurationPolicy: Exact duplicate STATdx exports are archived as optional duplicates; RadPrimer backbone images stay selected. Near duplicates, conceptual replacements, and alternate same-disease examples remain selected as recognition reinforcement unless visually identical or unusable.
sourceSelectionPlan.duplicateEvidencePolicy: ExactDuplicate decisions used the actual image_evidence files plus visual inspection and perceptual-hash distances. Caption-only or topic-only matches were not treated as exact duplicates.

caseClusterGuardrails.rule: Same-patient, follow-up, procedure, adjacent-slice, comparison-view, and time-lapse clusters are atomic teaching clusters.
caseClusterGuardrails.note: No source case cluster was intentionally split. RP-15 remains selected because STATdx lacks that 6-week follow-up timepoint.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial graphic depicts pathology of classic chronic subdural hematoma (SDH) with the formation of thin internal , thicker external membranes. Note the stretched bridging veins crossing the SDH, top-to-bottom gradation of fluid from serosanguinous to more proteinaceous contents.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.
